sections.paragraph:
γόνιμος, ον (also fem.-η) (Aristoph., Eur., Hippocr., pap, Philo, Joseph.) pert. to being productive (cp. γονή ‘offspring’) fruitful of birds and esp. of animals GJs 3:2 (3 times).—DELG s.v. γίγνομαι p. 223.

fem. fem. = feminine

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

esp. esp. = especially

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 205
